📌 Description
In this video I break down the five-layer stack I recommend for AI automation and AI engineering: backend, database, frontend, AI models, and infrastructure. I cover Python, FastAPI, Celery, Postgres, Supabase, React, Vite, and ShadCN UI as the core tools for building dashboards, internal tools, APIs, and background automation. I also explain the AI layer, including language models, embeddings, vision, speech, and image generation through API calls from providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. Finally, I discuss infrastructure options such as Docker, Railway, VPS deployment, and cloud hosting for shipping and running the full system end to

title: Build a Full-Stack GenAI Project in 4 Hours (FastAPI, React, Supabase)
description:
Want to start freelancing? Let me help: https://go.datalumina.com/rWzGBfV

⏱️ Timestamps
0:00 Full Stack GenAI Project Overview
4:32 Client Brief and SEC Data
18:45 Project Stack and Architecture
28:34 Implementation Plan and Environment Setup
43:42 Backend Foundation and Database
1:08:15 Frontend Auth and Chat
1:34:49 Ingestion Chunking and Embeddings

2:20:05 Frontend Integration and Citations
2:50:17 Deployment Readiness and UI Polish
2:59:50 Railway Deployment
3:34:28 Final App Walkthrough
3:45:00 Production Improvements and Wrap Up

📌 Description
In this full stack GenAI project tutorial, we build a Document Copilot from scratch: a RAG application that lets users ask questions over SEC filings with grounded answers, citations, and chat history. You will see the complete AI engineering workflow with FastAPI, Supabase Postgres, pgvector, React, TypeScript, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, document ingestion, embeddings, hybrid search, and Railway deployment. This end-to-end build shows how frontend, backend, database, ingestion pipeline, retrieval, and agentic AI workflows come together in a real production-style GenAI application.

title: If I Wanted to Build an AI SaaS in 2026, I’d Do This
description:
Want to start freelancing? Let me help: https://go.datalumina.com/P3ISUfG
Want to learn real AI Engineering? Go here: https://go.datalumina.com/eLy3l9R

🔗 Get started with Vanta: https://www.vanta.com/dave

Writing AI prompts all day?
🎙Try Glaido, the #1 voice dictation app for developers: https://get.glaido.com/dave

⏱️ Timestamps
00:00 Why Most Developers Build SaaS Backwards
01:19 The Real Game: Getting Customers to Pay and Stay
02:46 The Service to Software Ladder
03:22 Step 1: Done For Them
05:14 Start With Your Warm Network, Not Cold Outreach
07:53 Position Yourself as Curious, Not Salesy
08:46 Spotting Breakage: Painful, Repetitive, Costly, Unfixed
09:49 Proposing a Paid Proof of Concept
13:02 The "Turn It Off" Test
13:34 Example: Customer Support Automation
15:36 Step 2: Done With Them
18:19 Switching to Value-Based Pricing
19:46 Compliance Management (Vanta)
21:56 Step 3: Done By Them
24:07 Getting Customers to Value Fast
24:43 Distribution and Network Effects
26:44 Recap: You Can Stop at Any Level
27:55 Wrap Up and Next Steps

📌 Description
In this video, I lay out a three-step "Service-to-Software ladder” for building a B2B AI SaaS company without outside funding. Get paid first, prove value with one real customer, and then turn the work into scalable software. The first stage focuses on finding a painful, repetitive business problem in one company and solving it through discovery conversations and warm contacts instead of building a generic product. The second stage is “done with them,” where the solution is rebuilt for three to five similar customers, with niche targeting, value-based pricing, and customer introductions used to grow. The final stage is “done by them,” where the product becomes self-serve with onboarding and checkout.

title: Your Pip Install Is a Backdoor - Fix This Now!
description:
Want to learn real AI Engineering? Go here: https://go.datalumina.com/ZjMC0rq
Want to start freelancing? Let me help: https://go.datalumina.com/GUltjE7

Get Started with UV
https://youtu.be/5rTwOt9Qgik

⏱️ Timestamps
00:00 Supply Chain Attack Warning
03:15 Why Trust Is Dangerous
04:42 Three Python Safety Tips
08:26 Locked Sync Protects Projects
10:45 Make Dependencies Earn It

📌 Description
Learn how to protect your Python and JavaScript projects from supply chain attacks targeting NPM and PyPI packages, including phishing, stolen CI tokens, and lookalike package names that scrape SSH keys, API keys, and environment variables. Discover three practical defensive steps using uv: pinning exact dependency versions, excluding packages newer than 7 days, and enforcing locked sync behavior in CI/CD pipelines. Plus, learn how to safely manage AI coding agents like Claude Code to prevent automatic installation of compromised packages.

title: The Complete Guide to Hybrid Search in RAG (BM25 + Embeddings + Reranker)
description:
Want to learn real AI Engineering? Go here: https://go.datalumina.com/QpP01LX
Want to start freelancing? Let me help: https://go.datalumina.com/jOYILqO

🔗 GitHub Repository
https://github.com/daveebbelaar/ai-cookbook/tree/main/knowledge/hybrid-retrieval

🛠️ My VS Code / Cursor Setup
https://youtu.be/mpk4Q5feWaw

⏱️ Timestamps
00:00 Hybrid Retrieval Overview
01:00 Meet the Finance QA Data
03:29 Exploring Queries and Corpus
06:09 Mapping Questions to Documents
09:37 Retrieval Pipeline Roadmap
10:05 BM25 Keyword Retrieval
14:44 Tokenizing the Corpus
17:11 Building the BM25 Index
19:25 Querying with BM25
23:56 Why Dense Embeddings Help
25:13 Creating Dense Embeddings
32:11 Dense Search in Python
36:45 Dense Retrieval Compared
37:12 Reciprocal Rank Fusion
40:51 Fusing Search Results
43:56 Adding the Re-Ranker
46:44 Re-Ranking Hybrid Candidates
49:36 Evaluating Retrieval Quality
54:27 Tuning for Your Own Data

📌 Description
In this lecture, I build a production-style hybrid retrieval system from scratch, combining BM25, dense embeddings (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small), reciprocal rank fusion, and Cohere's re-ranker into a single pipeline. Using the FinanceQA dataset from the BEIR benchmark, I walk through each stage, loading and inspecting the corpus, building a BM25 index, generating dense embeddings, fusing rankings with RRF, and re-ranking the top candidates. The final section evaluates all four approaches with NDCG@10, showing how the full hybrid plus re-ranker stack outperforms each method on its own.

title: Building Agentic RAG From Scratch in Pure Python
description:

⏱️ Timestamps

00:48 Semantic vs. Agentic RAG
02:12 Tool Definitions and Setup
04:11 Listing Files
06:01 Searching for Patterns
10:19 Reading Files
12:37 Building the Agent
13:31 Debugging the Agent
18:34 Structured Output
20:33 Production Considerations
25:19 Conclusion and Next Steps

📌 Description
Learn how to build an agentic RAG system from scratch in pure Python without relying on heavy frameworks. This tutorial walks through creating custom tools for listing, searching, and reading markdown files, then connecting them to an AI agent using Pydantic AI for iterative tool calling and self-correction. Whether you're moving past basic semantic RAG or integrating private company data into LLMs, this hands-on guide covers the full workflow from core code to production deployment.

title: If I Started AI/ML Freelancing in 2026, I'd Do This
description:
Want to start freelancing? Let me help: https://go.datalumina.com/Kr4QzPA
Want to learn real AI Engineering? Go here: https://go.datalumina.com/babVFvV

⏱️ Timestamps
00:00 Freelance AI Business Blueprint
00:50 Getting Started with Freelancing
05:25 Transitioning to Paid Work
10:50 Scaling Your Freelance Career
12:47 Joining the Data Freelancer Program
14:10 Recap of the AfricAI Event
15:03 Final Thoughts on Freelancing

📌 Description
In this video I break down the exact three-part framework I'd follow to build a freelance AI business from scratch in 2026, based on the last six years I've spent freelancing full-time as an AI engineer and over $1.5M in signed contracts. You'll learn the Data Freelancer Blueprint: Get Going, Get Paid, and Get Good, the same system I've used to help hundreds of developers and data professionals start landing real client projects. I cover how to build your first portfolio projects, how to position yourself on LinkedIn, how to get on calls with decision makers, how to price proposals, and the secret most full-time freelance AI engineers won't tell you about anchor contracts. 

Whether you're a Python developer, a data engineer, or building with low-code tools like n8n, this freelance AI roadmap works across the stack. I also talk about the recent AfricAI event in Cape Town sponsored by @n8n-io  where I got together with Liam Ottley, Nate Herkelman, AI Jack and over 100 other AI builders to share what's actually working in AI services right now. If you're serious about starting a freelance AI career, going independent, or replacing your full-time income with high-ticket AI consulting work, this one's for you.

title: Programming Has Changed - Here's What I Focus On Now
description:
Want to start freelancing? Let me help: https://go.datalumina.com/qkAhBkh
Want to learn real AI Engineering? Go here: https://go.datalumina.com/eeW0N1e

Every company in tech is moving from AI-enabled to AI-first, and eventually AI-native, and most software engineers, AI engineers, and data scientists aren't ready for what that shift means for their careers. In this video I break down the three stages of AI adoption and explain why the biggest opportunity right now is learning to build AI agents that automate real business workflows, not just writing code. If you want to stay relevant as AI transforms the tech industry, your job as a developer is to help companies make this transition by combining full-stack engineering with a real understanding of how businesses operate.
